heroHeading:
What Does a Renewable Energy
Investor Actually Buy?
heroSubheading: Not a power plant — but the predictable cash flows it produces. AFA verifies that distinction with technical precision.
framingQuote: Can this project generate steady, reliable revenue for the next 10–20 years?
framingChecklist: Can the plant actually connect to the grid?
Can all generated electricity be sold, or will it be curtailed by the system?
Are the required permits complete and legally sound?
Will the expected revenue cover debt service and return targets?
framingClosing: The investor isn't buying the project — they're buying its capacity to generate durable, sustained revenue.
validationHeading:
Developers Present Optimistically.
Investors Require Independent Verification.
validationIntro: The investor views the project from the outside. That distance is an advantage — but the development team may present field risks more optimistically than reality warrants.
validationRisks: A connection permit exists, but actual grid connection is not feasible.
Curtailment risk is not fully reflected in revenue forecasts.
Permits exist, but the process sequence contains errors.
The schedule is unrealistic, and delays will disrupt cash flow.
validationWithout: Without technical verification: The investor may acquire risk without recognising it.
validationWith: With technical verification: The investor measures risk and manages it at the right price.
profilesHeading: Not All Investors Carry the Same Risk
profilesIntro: The same project can be suitable for one investor and too risky for another.

profiles.subtitle: Insurance companies, pension funds
thinking: Expects revenue to be as predictable as possible.
preferences: Structures with contracted revenue or protection mechanisms.
Projects with low and measurable curtailment risk.
Projects with completed or clearly closing permit processes.
profiles.thresholdDetail: Typically will not proceed if uncertainty exists. Even in a conditionally viable project, will wait if it is unclear how and when open items will be resolved.

profiles.subtitle: Mid-to-long term cash flow oriented professional funds
thinking: Wants cash flow but can conduct active management.
preferences: Scalable structures suitable for portfolio building.
Projects with analysed and manageable curtailment risk.
Plants with performance improvement potential during operations.
profiles.thresholdDetail: More open to conditionally viable projects, but will not proceed unless it is clear who will manage the risks and how they will be contractually secured.

profiles.subtitle: Higher return-seeking investors
thinking: Plans to create value and exit via sale or refinancing.
preferences: Projects in development or near ready-to-build stage.
Structures where value can be increased through improvement.
Projects with a clear exit story.
profiles.threshold: Risk must be closeable; price adjusted accordingly.
profiles.thresholdDetail: Most open to conditionally viable projects. However, if uncertainty cannot be resolved, will significantly reduce the price.

riskHeading: Why Isn't the Same Project Right for Every Investor?
riskIntro: What matters is not the risk itself, but its transferability.

riskRows.approach: Analyses uncertainty; accepts it if it can be made manageable.
Prices uncertainty; converts it to opportunity if it can be resolved.

riskClosing: A project is neither 'good' nor 'bad'. It is simply more suitable for a specific type of investor.
motivationHeading: What Every Investor Has in Common
motivationIntro: Regardless of investor type, all converge on four fundamental criteria.

motivations.title: Revenue Resilience
motivations.desc: The capacity to generate stable and predictable cash flows over the long term.

motivations.title: Measurable Risk
motivations.desc: The ability to characterise uncertainty within defined risk bands.

motivations.title: Viable Timeline
motivations.desc: Construction and commissioning schedules that are realistic and defensible.

motivations.title: Exit Capability
motivations.desc: A clear and actionable exit story for sale or refinancing.

motivationATRNote: AFA's ATR Matrix™ methodology evaluates every project against these four criteria and brings the risk profile to a level suitable for investor presentation.
ctaHeading:
Know the Investor Type
Before You Look at the Project.
ctaSubtext: AFA's filtered project pipeline presents projects with visible risk profiles and completed technical verification.
disclaimer: Project-specific legal and technical verification is recommended.
